concepts.name: Tree Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Visiting each node in a tree data structure exactly once.

concepts.name: Depth-First Search (DFS)
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.short_definition: A programming technique where a function calls itself to solve smaller instances of the same problem.

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that follows the Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) principle.

concepts.name: Pre-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Visiting the root node first, then recursively traversing the left subtree, and finally the right subtree.

concepts.name: In-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Recursively traversing the left subtree, visiting the root node, and then recursively traversing the right subtree.

concepts.name: Post-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Recursively traversing the left subtree, then the right subtree, and finally visiting the root node.

concepts.name: Iterative DFS
concepts.short_definition: Implementing Depth-First Search using an explicit stack data structure instead of recursion.

concepts.name: Tree Node
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental component of a tree data structure, typically containing data and references to child nodes.

concepts.name: Binary Tree
concepts.short_definition: A tree data structure where each node has at most two children, referred to as the left child and the right child.
